title: Garlicky Fluffy Thai Pork Moo Ping
description: A simple yet flavorful Central Thai dish made with pork, garlic, and a special type of sauce served on a bed of fluffy pappardelle pasta. A midnight craving's best friend.
instructions: Whisk together fish sauce, palm sugar, soy sauce, and 2 cloves of minced garlic in a large bowl.
Add the pork shoulder to the marinade and mix until well coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the marinated pork shoulder to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Transfer the cooked pork to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and cook the remaining 3 cloves of minced garlic until fragrant.
Add the cooked pappardelle pasta to the skillet and toss to combine with the garlic and oil.
Slice the cooked pork into thin strips and add to the pasta.
Serve the Moo Ping hot, garnished with chopped cilantro if desired.
